ALL SPECIALTIES / PHYSICAL THERAPIST / DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

District of Columbia Physical Therapist License

District of Columbia Physical Therapist license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Physical Therapist applications in District of Columbia run through DC Board of Physical Therapy. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Transcripts - Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report - Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 6 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

  1. STEP 1

    Create Account on DC Health Licensing Portal

    Register for a new user account on the DC Health Licensing Portal at hrla.doh.dc.gov/hrla/default.asp if you do not already have one. You will need to verify your email address during this process.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Complete Online Application

    Log in to your newly created or existing account, select the 'Physical Therapist' application type (either 'By Examination' for new graduates or 'By Endorsement' for out-of-state licensees), and accurately complete all required sections of the application form.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Submit Supporting Documentation

    Upload official transcripts verifying completion of an accredited physical therapy program. Arrange for official NPTE scores to be sent directly to the Board. If applying by endorsement, provide verification of licensure from all other states or jurisdictions where you have held a PT license.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)

    Follow the specific instructions provided within the DC Health Licensing Portal or by the Board of Physical Therapy to complete the mandatory criminal background check.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Pay Application Fees

    Submit the non-refundable application fee of $200 (for both initial and endorsement applications) and any other associated fees through the DC Health Licensing Portal using a credit/debit card.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Board Review and Licensure

    The Board of Physical Therapy will review your complete application and all supporting documentation. Upon approval and verification that all requirements are met, your Physical Therapist license will be issued.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$200
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$200

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
DC Board of Physical Therapy
Phone
(202) 442-5955
Website
dchealth.dc.gov/service/physical-therapy-licensing
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Physical Therapist renewal in District of Columbia follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$170
Continuing education
30 hours every 2 years

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a District of Columbia physical therapist license cost?

For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $200. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does District of Columbia have reciprocity for physical therapists?

No interstate compact currently covers physical therapist practice in District of Columbia. To work in District of Columbia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to District of Columbia?

Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

Can I start working in District of Columbia while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$200

Processing time

4 to 6 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE)

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years · $170

Licensing board

DC Board of Physical Therapy

dchealth.dc.gov/service/physical-therapy-licensing

(202) 442-5955

This page is maintained as a reference for physical therapists and recruiters evaluating District of Columbia assignments.
